A proposed law to To allocate substantial federal funding for the nationwide replacement of lead service lines in drinking water systems, prioritizing environmental justice communities and schools, with mandatory timelines for completion by 2035 and annual reporting requirements to Congress on progress and health impact assessments.

Legislation to comprehensively reform the federal student loan system by introducing income-based repayment plans with automatic forgiveness after 10 years of qualifying payments, eliminating interest accrual during enrollment, and establishing a one-time debt relief program for borrowers who have repaid for 20 years or more without default.

Legislation to to establish a National Commission on Gun Violence Prevention, composed of experts in public health, law enforcement, mental health, and community leaders, tasked with producing a comprehensive report within 18 months including evidence-based policy recommendations on background checks, red flag laws, safe storage requirements, and assault weapons restrictions.

To amend the Clean Air Act to impose stricter national ambient air quality standards for fine particulate matter (PM2.5) and ozone, require accelerated phase-down of hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs), and provide transition assistance grants to industries affected by the updated regulations.

A proposed law to To create a federal paid family and medical leave insurance program funded through a shared payroll contribution between employers and employees, providing up to 12 weeks of partial wage replacement for bonding with a new child, caring for a seriously ill family member, or personal serious health conditions, with job protection provisions.

Legislation to strengthen antitrust enforcement by lowering thresholds for merger reviews, creating a presumption against mergers that significantly increase market concentration, empowering state attorneys general to challenge mergers, and increasing penalties for anticompetitive conduct by dominant digital platforms.

Legislation to to expand Medicare to cover comprehensive dental, vision, and hearing services for all beneficiaries, with phased implementation starting with low-income seniors, and funding through a combination of negotiated drug prices savings and a modest premium adjustment for higher-income enrollees.

To authorize appropriations exceeding $50 billion over five years for domestic semiconductor manufacturing, advanced packaging facilities, workforce training programs, and research and development in next-generation chip technologies under an expanded CHIPS and Science Act framework.

An act to To establish a federal right-to-repair law requiring manufacturers of consumer electronics, agricultural equipment, medical devices, and motor vehicles to provide owners and independent repair shops with access to parts, tools, diagnostic software, and repair manuals at fair market prices.

Legislation to create a National Climate Resilience Fund, capitalized at $100 billion over ten years, to support state, local, and tribal governments in funding infrastructure hardening, flood mapping updates, wildfire mitigation, coastal protection, and community relocation projects in high-risk areas.

A proposed law to to reform qualified immunity doctrine for law enforcement officers by removing it as a defense in excessive force and unlawful search cases unless the officer can demonstrate that their conduct was explicitly authorized by clearly established law at the time of the incident.

To require all large publicly traded corporations to annually disclose their effective federal tax rate, country-by-country tax payments, and any use of offshore tax havens, with public reporting requirements and penalties for non-compliance enforced by the Securities and Exchange Commission.

An act to To invest $75 billion in a Green Jobs Corps program providing paid training, apprenticeships, and employment opportunities in clean energy installation, energy efficiency retrofits, electric vehicle manufacturing, and environmental restoration for young adults aged 18-35, with priority for disadvantaged communities.

Legislation to protect reproductive health care access by codifying the right to contraception, emergency abortion care, and interstate travel for reproductive services, while prohibiting federal funding restrictions on providers that offer comprehensive reproductive health services.

Legislation to to establish a federal database of police use-of-force incidents, misconduct complaints, and disciplinary actions, requiring mandatory reporting by all law enforcement agencies receiving federal funding, with public access provisions subject to privacy protections for individuals.

To amend immigration law to increase annual family-based and employment-based visa caps, eliminate per-country limits that create decades-long backlogs, provide a pathway to citizenship for long-term undocumented residents meeting character and tax compliance requirements, and expand protections for farmworkers.

Draft law to To fund accelerated research, development, demonstration, and deployment of advanced nuclear reactor technologies, including small modular reactors and microreactors, through public-private partnerships, loan guarantees, and a dedicated R&D center of excellence at national laboratories.

Legislation to prohibit the use of algorithmic decision-making systems in high-stakes contexts (employment, housing, credit, education, criminal justice) unless they have been independently audited for bias, fairness, and disparate impact, with mandatory disclosure of system performance metrics.

A proposed law to to expand the child tax credit to $3,600 per child under age 6 and $3,000 for children 6-17, make it fully refundable and monthly, and index it to inflation, with phase-outs starting at higher income thresholds to target middle- and working-class families.

To create a federal program providing matching grants to states that adopt automatic voter registration systems integrated with motor vehicle departments, public assistance agencies, and other government touchpoints, aiming for 90% registration rates among eligible citizens by 2032.

Presidential directive directing all federal agencies to achieve net-zero greenhouse gas emissions from operations, buildings, fleets, and supply chains by 2040 at the latest, with interim targets of 50% reduction by 2030, and requiring annual public progress reports with third-party verification.

Cabinet order establishing a National Climate Adaptation Strategy Implementation Task Force chaired by the White House Climate Advisor, coordinating across agencies to prioritize resilience investments, update vulnerability assessments every five years, and integrate adaptation into all federal permitting and funding decisions.

It is hereby ordered that the federal government shall transition its entire non-tactical vehicle fleet to zero-emission vehicles by 2035, with accelerated procurement of electric and hydrogen fuel cell vehicles, charging infrastructure deployment at federal facilities, and workforce training programs for maintenance personnel.

Cabinet order on Advancing Environmental Justice and Cumulative Impact Assessments, requiring agencies to identify and address disproportionate environmental and health burdens on overburdened communities before approving major infrastructure, energy, or permitting actions.

Order requiring federal contractors and grantees with contracts over $250,000 to adopt comprehensive diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility plans, conduct annual pay equity audits, and report workforce demographic data publicly, with preference in future awards for demonstrated progress.

Order by the President directing the Office of Management and Budget to develop government-wide standards for responsible artificial intelligence procurement, use, and monitoring, including mandatory impact assessments, human scrutiny requirements, and prohibitions on high-risk uses without safeguards.

By the authority vested in me as President, federal agencies shall prioritize procurement from businesses owned by underrepresented groups, veterans, and persons with disabilities, with new set-aside goals of 15% for small disadvantaged businesses and 5% for service-disabled veteran-owned firms by fiscal year 2030.

White House order on Strengthening Supply Chain Transparency and Resilience, requiring critical sector companies receiving federal funds to map Tier 1 and Tier 2 suppliers, disclose single points of failure, and develop contingency plans for geopolitical, climate, and cyber disruptions.

Order establishing an Interagency Council on Emerging Biotechnology Governance to coordinate regulation, funding, and risk assessment of synthetic biology, gene editing, and engineered organisms, ensuring benefits are realized while preventing misuse and environmental harm.

White House order to combat foreign malign influence operations by enhancing cybersecurity defenses for election infrastructure, requiring social media platforms to label state-sponsored content, and creating a rapid-response fusion center for disinformation threats during election periods.

A proposed law to To allocate more than $200 billion over the next decade for a comprehensive national infrastructure resilience initiative, including upgrades to aging bridges, dams, levees, and power grids to withstand increasing extreme weather events driven by climate change, with mandatory vulnerability assessments for all federally funded projects and priority funding directed toward historically disadvantaged and low-income communities facing disproportionate risks.

Legislation to overhaul the federal tax code by closing loopholes that benefit multinational corporations through offshore profit shifting, implementing a minimum effective corporate tax rate of 21% on book income, expanding the base erosion and anti-abuse tax (BEAT), and using resulting revenues to fund universal pre-kindergarten, community college tuition assistance, and expanded child care subsidies for working families.

A proposed law to to establish a Federal Commission on Artificial Intelligence Governance and Ethics, comprised of representatives from government, academia, industry, civil society, and labor organizations, charged with developing binding national standards for high-risk AI systems within 24 months, including requirements for transparency, explainability, bias mitigation, human monitoring, and liability frameworks for harms caused by autonomous decision-making.

To amend the Social Security Act to gradually raise the payroll tax cap over 10 years until 90% of earnings are subject to taxation, introduce progressive benefit adjustments for high earners, and establish an automatic stabilizer mechanism that temporarily increases benefits during economic downturns to support retirees and disabled individuals.

A proposed law to To create a National Strategy for Ocean and Great Lakes Conservation and Restoration, authorizing $50 billion in dedicated funding over 15 years for habitat protection, coral reef and mangrove restoration, sustainable fisheries management, plastic pollution reduction programs, offshore wind compatible with marine ecosystems, and blue carbon sequestration initiatives to combat ocean acidification and biodiversity loss.

Legislation to comprehensively reform the federal criminal justice system by ending mandatory minimum sentences for non-violent drug offenses, expanding compassionate release and clemency opportunities, investing heavily in reentry programs including housing, employment, and mental health services, and creating a federal expungement process for certain low-level convictions after a waiting period.

Legislation to to establish universal, high-quality, publicly funded child care and early learning programs accessible to all families regardless of income, with sliding-scale fees capped at 7% of household income, staffed by well-compensated early educators, and integrated with existing Head Start and state pre-K systems to ensure seamless transitions and developmental continuity.

To require all federal agencies and contractors with annual revenues exceeding $1 billion to publicly disclose their full Scope 1, 2, and 3 greenhouse gas emissions annually, set science-based reduction targets aligned with limiting global warming to 1.5°C, and face financial penalties or loss of federal contracts for failure to demonstrate meaningful progress toward net-zero by 2050.

A proposed law to To invest $150 billion in a Civilian Climate Corps modeled after the New Deal-era Civilian Conservation Corps, providing paid training and employment for up to 500,000 young adults and displaced workers annually in high-impact climate mitigation and adaptation jobs such as reforestation, urban greening, wetland restoration, renewable energy installation, and disaster preparedness.

Legislation to protect digital privacy rights by enacting comprehensive federal data protection standards that include the right to access, correct, delete, and port personal data, strict limits on targeted advertising based on sensitive categories, mandatory data minimization and purpose limitation, algorithmic impact assessments, and a private right of action for violations enforced by a strengthened Federal Trade Commission and state attorneys general.

An act to to reform campaign finance laws by establishing public matching funds for small-dollar donations up to $200 at a 6:1 ratio, lowering contribution limits for PACs and super PACs, requiring real-time online disclosure of donors giving more than $200, and prohibiting foreign-influenced corporations from spending in U.S. elections.

To authorize a major expansion of the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) combined with weatherization assistance and electrification rebates, aiming to eliminate energy burden exceeding 6% of income for low-income households through targeted retrofits, heat pump installations, and utility bill assistance tied to energy efficiency improvements.

A proposed law to To establish a federal right to internet access as essential infrastructure, directing the FCC to develop and implement a Universal Broadband Service Obligation requiring providers to offer affordable, reliable high-speed service (at least 100/20 Mbps) in all areas, with subsidies for low-income households and public investment in last-mile infrastructure in unserved regions.

Legislation to strengthen labor rights by passing the Protecting the Right to Organize (PRO) Act, banning state right-to-work laws, protecting gig workers' ability to organize, imposing civil penalties for employer interference, and streamlining union certification processes while enhancing remedies for unfair labor practices.

Legislation to to create a National Fund for Community Violence Intervention and Prevention, providing sustained federal grants to evidence-based programs employing credible messengers, hospital-based violence interrupters, street outreach, conflict mediation, and youth mentorship in high-violence urban and rural communities.

To amend federal law to require pharmaceutical companies to publicly justify price increases above the rate of medical inflation for drugs receiving federal support through research funding, tax credits, or Medicare/Medicaid reimbursement, with automatic rebates triggered for excessive increases and authority for direct government negotiation on high-cost drugs.

Legislation to To fund a comprehensive national reforestation and afforestation initiative targeting 1 billion additional trees planted by 2035 on public, private, and tribal lands, with priority for degraded ecosystems, urban heat islands, post-wildfire landscapes, and areas vulnerable to flooding, incorporating native species and community involvement.

Legislation to protect whistleblowers across all sectors by expanding coverage under the Whistleblower Protection Act to private-sector employees in critical infrastructure, creating a centralized Office of Whistleblower Affairs, increasing rewards for successful qui tam actions, and prohibiting retaliation through broad anti-gag order provisions.

An act to to establish a Federal Office of Gun Violence Prevention within the Department of Health and Human Services, coordinating public health approaches including research funding, community-based prevention grants, extreme risk protection orders, safe storage mandates, and trauma-informed support for survivors and at-risk individuals.

To reform the federal permitting process for clean energy, transmission, and critical minerals projects by establishing statutory deadlines, one-stop-shop coordination, concurrent environmental reviews under NEPA, and community benefit agreements requirements to accelerate deployment while maintaining strong environmental and cultural protections.

White House order directing immediate federal action to achieve 100% carbon pollution-free electricity on federal facilities and operations by 2030 through aggressive on-site renewables, power purchase agreements, efficiency upgrades, and electrification of heating and transportation systems, with mandatory annual reporting and independent audits.

White House order establishing a government-wide Climate Risk Disclosure and Management Framework requiring every federal agency to integrate physical and transition climate risks into strategic planning, budgeting, procurement, grant-making, and regulatory decisions, including scenario analysis aligned with IPCC pathways.

It is hereby ordered that all new and replacement federal buildings and major renovations shall be designed and constructed to net-zero emissions standards, with high-performance energy efficiency, on-site renewable generation where feasible, and resilience features to withstand projected climate extremes through at least 2070.

Order by the President on Advancing Indigenous Knowledge and Co-Stewardship in Federal Land and Resource Management, requiring meaningful tribal consultation, incorporation of traditional ecological knowledge in decision-making, and co-management agreements for protected areas and natural resources affecting tribal interests.

Order mandating that federal agencies prioritize procurement of low-carbon, circular economy products and services, including requirements for recycled content, extended producer responsibility considerations, and lifecycle greenhouse gas assessments in major acquisitions over $10 million.

White House order to accelerate the clean energy transition in federal fleets and facilities by setting binding interim and final targets for zero-emission vehicle adoption, mandating installation of charging and refueling infrastructure, and providing workforce transition support for affected employees.

By the authority vested in me, federal agencies shall develop and implement comprehensive plans to address environmental justice communities disproportionately burdened by pollution, siting of hazardous facilities, and climate impacts, including cumulative risk assessments and targeted investments in mitigation and health protections.

White House order on Strengthening Democratic Resilience Against Disinformation and Foreign Influence, directing enhanced coordination among intelligence, law enforcement, and regulatory agencies to monitor, attribute, and counter inauthentic behavior on digital platforms during election periods and national security events.

Order establishing a National Commission on the Future of Work and Automation, tasked with examining impacts of AI, robotics, and technological change on employment, wages, inequality, and social safety nets, and recommending policy responses including universal basic services, reskilling investments, and updated labor protections.

Presidential directive directing agencies to adopt open data and open-source software policies by default, requiring publication of non-sensitive government data in machine-readable formats and prioritizing open-source solutions in procurement unless proprietary alternatives demonstrate clear superiority.

Legislation proposing a comprehensive overhaul of the federal housing policy, including expansion of rental assistance vouchers to cover all eligible low-income households, incentives for landlords to accept vouchers, and substantial investments in new affordable housing construction through public-private partnerships and community land trusts to address the nationwide shortage estimated at over 7 million units.

A proposal to enact nationwide standards for workplace safety in extreme heat conditions, mandating employers to provide paid rest breaks, access to shade and hydration, heat illness prevention training, and emergency response protocols, with enhanced enforcement authority for the Occupational Safety and Health Administration and civil penalties scaled to company size.

To introduce a federal framework for regulating cryptocurrency and digital asset markets, establishing licensing requirements for exchanges and custodians, implementing consumer protection rules against fraud and theft, requiring transparent reserve audits for stablecoins, and creating a joint regulatory task force between the SEC and CFTC to clarify jurisdictional boundaries.

Measures to accelerate the transition to electric and zero-emission heavy-duty vehicles by providing purchase incentives, infrastructure grants for charging and hydrogen stations along major freight corridors, workforce retraining for mechanics and drivers, and phased emission standards for new trucks and buses starting in model year 2029.

A legislative package aimed at revitalizing rural America through targeted investments in broadband expansion, small business incubators, agricultural innovation research, rural health clinics, and value-added processing facilities to reduce economic dependence on commodity agriculture and stem population decline in non-metro counties.

Provisions to strengthen federal review of private equity firms in the health care sector by requiring transparency in ownership structures, limiting excessive debt loading on acquired hospitals and nursing homes, and imposing accountability measures when patient outcomes or staffing levels deteriorate post-acquisition.

An initiative to establish a federal program supporting community-based renewable energy cooperatives, offering low-interest loans, technical assistance, and tax credits for projects owned and governed by local residents, with priority for areas historically underserved by utility-scale development.

Reforms to modernize the federal civil service system by streamlining hiring processes, expanding flexible work arrangements, improving performance management, and creating career pathways for underrepresented groups while preserving merit-based principles and protections against political interference.

A comprehensive plan to address maternal mortality and morbidity disparities by expanding Medicaid postpartum coverage to at least one year, funding doula and midwife services, investing in implicit bias training for providers, and establishing a national maternal health data registry with real-time monitoring and intervention triggers.

Legislation directing the establishment of a National Center for Missing and Exploited Children task force dedicated to combating online child sexual exploitation material, enhancing coordination with tech platforms for rapid content removal, and supporting victim-centered investigations and prosecutions.

White House order mandating that all new federal financial assistance programs incorporate explicit equity screening criteria, requiring applicants to demonstrate how proposed activities will reduce or avoid exacerbating existing disparities based on race, ethnicity, income, geography, and disability status, with mandatory annual equity impact reports.

Cabinet order directing the creation of a Federal Interagency Working Group on Extreme Heat Preparedness and Response, tasked with developing national heat action plans, coordinating early warning systems, funding cooling centers in vulnerable communities, and integrating heat risk into occupational safety standards and public health guidance.

It is hereby ordered that federal agencies shall prioritize the procurement and deployment of nature-based solutions—such as green infrastructure, wetland restoration, urban tree canopy expansion, and coastal marsh buffers—for flood risk reduction, water quality improvement, and carbon sequestration where technically and economically feasible.

White House order on Advancing Responsible Federal Use of Artificial Intelligence in Public Services, requiring agencies to conduct algorithmic impact assessments, ensure meaningful human monitoring in high-stakes decisions, establish redress mechanisms for individuals affected by automated decisions, and publish transparency reports on AI systems in use.

Order requiring all federal departments and agencies to develop and publicly release annual climate adaptation plans that identify mission-critical vulnerabilities to physical climate risks, outline specific adaptation measures with timelines and budgets, and demonstrate alignment with the most recent National Climate Assessment projections.

Cabinet order establishing a government-wide initiative to eliminate single-use plastics in federal operations by 2030, including bans on plastic water bottles and foodware in federal facilities, requirements for sustainable packaging in procurement contracts, and support for innovation in biodegradable alternatives through SBIR/STTR funding.

By the authority vested in me, federal agencies shall expand telehealth and remote service delivery models across programs, with particular emphasis on rural, tribal, and underserved urban areas, while ensuring equitable access through broadband subsidies, device distribution, and digital literacy support for beneficiaries.

Presidential directive to strengthen federal coordination on antimicrobial resistance by directing development of a updated National Action Plan, increasing funding for surveillance, research, and antibiotic stewardship programs, and requiring federal health care facilities to report resistance data and adopt best practices.

Order creating an Interagency Council on Food System Resilience to address vulnerabilities exposed by supply chain disruptions, climate impacts, and pandemics, with goals to diversify production regions, invest in local and regional food systems, reduce food waste, and improve nutritional security for low-income households.

Cabinet order directing agencies to integrate biodiversity considerations into all federal decision-making processes, including permitting, land management, infrastructure projects, and international assistance, with mandatory biodiversity impact assessments and no-net-loss policies where feasible.

Provisions for establishing a national registry of certified sustainable fisheries and aquaculture operations, providing market access preferences for U.S. seafood products meeting environmental and labor standards.

Initiative to create federal tax credits for homeowners and renters who install energy-efficient appliances, insulation, windows, and HVAC systems, combined with on-bill financing options through utilities.
Reforms aimed at reducing recidivism through expanded access to education, job training, substance use treatment, and housing support for individuals reentering society after incarceration.

A package to support small and mid-size farmers by increasing crop insurance subsidies for diversified operations, funding local food system infrastructure, and providing technical assistance for climate-smart agriculture practices.

Legislation directing the creation of a federal program to assist states in modernizing election administration systems, including secure paper ballot backups, risk-limiting audits, and accessible voting options for disabled voters.
Proposals to strengthen federal consumer protections against predatory lending by capping interest rates on payday loans, auto title loans, and deposit advance products nationwide.

An effort to fund expanded research and surveillance of emerging infectious diseases, including vector-borne illnesses, zoonotic pathogens, and antimicrobial-resistant bacteria, with dedicated laboratories and global early-warning networks.
Measures to promote domestic production of active pharmaceutical ingredients and generic drugs by providing grants, low-interest loans, and tax incentives for onshoring manufacturing facilities.
Legislation establishing a federal task force on elder financial exploitation, coordinating law enforcement, financial institutions, adult protective services, and consumer education campaigns.
Initiative to invest in urban forestry programs that plant and maintain trees in heat-vulnerable neighborhoods, with goals to increase canopy cover by 20% in disadvantaged urban areas by 2035.
Provisions for creating a national apprenticeship expansion grant program targeting underrepresented groups in construction, IT, health care, and advanced manufacturing trades.
A framework to require large corporations to document on workforce diversity metrics, pay equity analyses, and supplier diversity spending annually, with incentives for demonstrated progress.
Reforms to update federal wildfire management policy by increasing investments in hazardous fuels reduction, prescribed burning, community defensible space grants, and post-fire recovery support.
Legislation to expand federal support for arts and cultural organizations in rural and underserved communities through competitive grants and tax incentives for private donations.
Measures aimed at improving air quality in ports and freight corridors by funding zero-emission drayage trucks, shore power for ships, and electrified cargo handling equipment.
An initiative to establish federal standards for safe drinking water in schools and child care facilities, requiring regular testing for lead, PFAS, and other contaminants with remediation funding.

Proposals to support tribal sovereignty by increasing funding for tribal courts, law enforcement, health services, and infrastructure while strengthening consultation requirements on federal actions affecting tribal lands.

Legislation directing the development of a national strategy for electric vehicle battery recycling and second-life applications, including grants for domestic recycling facilities and workforce training.
A package to expand federal funding for mental health crisis intervention teams, mobile response units, and peer support specialists embedded in communities.
Presidential directive requiring federal agencies to conduct annual equity audits of their programs, grants, and regulations to identify and address barriers faced by underserved populations.

White House order establishing mandatory sustainability reporting for federal contractors above a certain threshold, including greenhouse gas emissions, water use, waste generation, and labor practices.

It is hereby ordered that all federal land management agencies prioritize restoration of degraded ecosystems through large-scale reforestation, wetland recovery, and invasive species removal projects.
White House order directing accelerated adoption of digital identity verification systems that protect privacy, reduce fraud, and improve access to government services for underserved groups.
Order mandating that federal health programs incorporate social determinants of health screening tools and referrals to community resources as standard practice.

Presidential directive on enhancing federal coordination to combat human trafficking, including increased victim services funding, training for first responders, and partnerships with NGOs and tech companies.

An act to require large online platforms to implement robust age-verification systems and child safety features, with independent audits and fines for failures to protect minors from harmful content.

Reforms to update federal antitrust laws for the platform economy, lowering thresholds for breaking up dominant firms and prohibiting self-preferencing practices in app stores and search engines.
Provisions for a national strategy to combat loneliness and social isolation, funding community programs, senior centers, volunteer matching, and mental health integration in primary care.

Initiative to provide federal matching grants and technical assistance to states adopting automatic voter registration systems integrated with motor vehicle departments, public assistance offices, and other government touchpoints, aiming to achieve near-universal registration rates among eligible citizens while incorporating robust security measures and accessibility features for disabled voters.

Package expanding labor protections to gig economy workers by including them in federal minimum wage, overtime, and collective bargaining rights, creating portable benefits platforms for health insurance and retirement, prohibiting misclassification, and establishing a dedicated enforcement unit to address violations while preserving platform flexibility.

Effort to substantially increase federal funding for arts and humanities programs, including competitive grants for museums, performing arts organizations, cultural heritage preservation, and community-based initiatives in rural, tribal, and underserved urban areas to promote cultural diversity, economic vitality, and educational enrichment.

Legislation creating a dedicated federal task force on youth vaping, tobacco, and nicotine prevention, coordinating public education campaigns, enforcing marketing restrictions near schools, restricting flavored products, funding cessation resources, and supporting research into long-term health impacts to reduce adolescent addiction rates.

Measures to accelerate the deployment of advanced small modular nuclear reactors at federal sites and in utility partnerships by providing loan guarantees, streamlined licensing, R&D funding, and workforce training to deliver clean, reliable baseload power and support decarbonization goals.

Initiative requiring large digital platforms to conduct independent annual audits of their recommendation and moderation algorithms for bias, discrimination, and unintended societal harms, with public disclosure of findings and corrective action plans enforced by federal regulators.

Reforms to establish federal matching grants encouraging states to expand paid sick leave coverage to part-time, temporary, and gig workers with accrual rates, job protection, and administrative support to improve worker health and economic security.

Package modernizing federal permitting processes for high-voltage transmission lines, renewable energy facilities, and critical infrastructure projects through one-stop coordination, concurrent environmental reviews under NEPA, statutory deadlines, and mandatory community benefit agreements to expedite clean energy deployment.

Cabinet order directing all federal land management agencies to meaningfully incorporate Indigenous traditional ecological knowledge into planning, decision-making, and restoration projects, with strengthened tribal consultation protocols and co-stewardship agreements for protected areas and natural resources.

Presidential directive establishing mandatory sustainability performance targets for all federal buildings and facilities, including specific reductions in energy consumption, water use, waste generation, and embodied carbon in materials, with annual public reporting and independent verification mechanisms.

It is hereby ordered that federal procurement policies prioritize domestically sourced critical minerals, processed materials, and manufactured components to enhance supply chain security for clean energy, defense, and health technologies.

Order mandating federal agencies to identify and address skills gaps in emerging technologies through workforce planning, targeted recruitment, reskilling programs, and partnerships with educational institutions in AI, quantum computing, cybersecurity, and biotechnology.

Measures to comprehensively update federal wildfire policy by allocating increased funding for proactive hazardous fuels reduction on federal, state, and private lands, expanding the use of prescribed and cultural burning practices informed by tribal expertise, providing competitive grants to communities for home hardening and defensible space creation, and establishing multi-year recovery funds dedicated to ecosystem restoration, community rebuilding, and economic diversification in wildfire-impacted regions.

Legislation directing the development and implementation of a National Electric Vehicle Charging Network Plan that requires fast-charging stations to be installed at intervals of no more than 50 miles along all interstate highways and major U.S. routes, offers substantial federal matching grants to states and private entities for deployment in rural, tribal, and low-income urban areas, mandates common technical standards for interoperability, and includes dedicated workforce training programs for installation, maintenance, and grid integration technicians.

Initiative providing generous tax credits, matching grants, and administrative support to employers who voluntarily expand or establish paid family and medical leave policies, with benefits structured on a sliding scale based on company size and employee wages, guaranteed job protection during approved leave periods, and flexible options for small businesses to participate without undue financial burden, aimed at improving family economic stability and workforce attachment.

Package of reforms prioritizing and incentivizing federal procurement from domestic manufacturers of strategically important technologies, including semiconductors, advanced batteries, rare earth processing equipment, and critical medical supplies, through expanded set-aside goals, domestic content thresholds, supply chain resilience requirements, and targeted incentives for onshoring production to strengthen national security and reduce exposure to global disruptions.

Effort to dramatically increase federal funding for comprehensive school-based mental health services by awarding competitive grants to school districts for hiring additional counselors, psychologists, and social workers, deploying tele-mental health platforms for remote access, providing mandatory trauma-informed training to all school personnel, and forging partnerships with local community providers to deliver early intervention, crisis response, and ongoing support for students dealing with anxiety, depression, trauma, or behavioral challenges.

Provisions requiring all publicly traded companies to include detailed climate-related financial risk disclosures in annual SEC filings, covering physical risks from extreme weather events, transition risks associated with policy shifts toward net-zero, and potential liability exposures, using standardized reporting templates aligned with international standards to enhance investor decision-making and promote market stability in a changing climate environment.

Legislation establishing a National Bio-Manufacturing and Biodefense Initiative to build resilient domestic capacity for producing vaccines, therapeutics, biologics, and advanced biomaterials, through a combination of direct grants, low-interest loans, tax incentives for facility construction, workforce training partnerships, and strategic stockpiling mechanisms to safeguard public health during pandemics or other biological threats.

An act to create baseline federal consumer data privacy rights, including the right to access, correct, delete, and port personal information held by companies, strict restrictions on targeted advertising using sensitive data categories, mandatory data minimization and purpose limitation principles, requirements for algorithmic impact assessments in high-risk systems, and robust enforcement mechanisms shared between the Federal Trade Commission and state attorneys general, with provisions for individual private rights of action.

Measures to initiate a nationwide lead hazard abatement and prevention program targeting older residential housing, schools, child care centers, and public facilities, providing full funding for remediation, temporary relocation support during abatement work, mandatory testing and certification protocols, long-term blood lead level monitoring, and community education campaigns to eliminate childhood lead exposure and mitigate associated neurological and developmental risks.

Initiative offering federal matching grants, technical assistance, and cybersecurity support to states implementing automatic voter registration systems integrated with driver licensing, public benefits applications, and other government interactions, designed to achieve near-universal registration among eligible citizens while incorporating strong security safeguards, accessibility accommodations for disabled voters, and regular independent audits to maintain election integrity.

Reforms allocating significant federal resources to coastal resilience and adaptation infrastructure, including nature-based approaches such as restored wetlands, living shorelines, and mangrove buffers, combined with engineered solutions like elevated roadways and storm surge barriers in vulnerable communities, emphasizing equity in funding allocation, mandatory community engagement, and integration into all federal permitting and disaster recovery processes to address accelerating sea level rise and intensifying storms.

Package extending core federal labor protections to gig economy and platform workers by including them under minimum wage, overtime, and collective bargaining provisions, establishing portable benefits frameworks for health coverage and retirement savings, prohibiting systematic worker misclassification, and creating a specialized enforcement division to investigate violations and provide remedies while preserving innovation in flexible work arrangements.

Effort to substantially boost federal funding for arts, humanities, and cultural programs through expanded competitive grants to museums, theaters, community arts organizations, and heritage preservation projects, with targeted support for rural, tribal, and historically underserved urban areas to foster cultural diversity, stimulate local economies, and enhance educational opportunities through creative expression.

Proposals to enhance federal scrutiny and consumer safeguards in cryptocurrency custody, exchange, and wallet services by mandating comprehensive insurance coverage, frequent third-party reserve audits, strict anti-fraud measures, clear asset segregation rules, and coordinated regulatory guidance between the Securities and Exchange Commission and Commodity Futures Trading Commission to balance innovation with investor protection and financial system stability.

Legislation forming a dedicated interagency task force on youth nicotine and vaping prevention, responsible for coordinating nationwide public education campaigns, enforcing restrictions on flavored products and marketing near schools, funding evidence-based cessation programs, supporting longitudinal health impact research, and collaborating with states, schools, and health providers to reverse rising adolescent addiction trends.

Measures to fast-track the siting, licensing, and deployment of advanced small modular nuclear reactors at federal facilities and in utility partnerships by providing streamlined regulatory pathways, loan guarantees, targeted R&D funding, workforce development programs, and community engagement requirements to deliver safe, reliable, low-carbon baseload power in support of national decarbonization objectives.

Initiative mandating large digital platforms and social media companies to perform independent, annual third-party audits of their content recommendation, moderation, and ranking algorithms for evidence of bias, discrimination, disinformation amplification, or other societal harms, with mandatory public summaries of findings and enforceable corrective action timelines overseen by federal regulators.

Reforms establishing federal matching grant incentives for states to broaden paid sick leave coverage to include part-time, temporary, seasonal, and gig workers, with minimum accrual rates, job protection during illness, administrative simplification for employers, and federal technical assistance to ensure widespread adoption and improved worker health security.

Package streamlining federal permitting and environmental review processes for high-priority clean energy infrastructure, including transmission lines, large-scale solar and wind facilities, and battery storage projects, through one-stop federal coordination, concurrent NEPA reviews, statutory deadlines, and required community benefit agreements to reduce delays while preserving strong environmental safeguards.

Order by the President instructing federal land management and environmental agencies to systematically integrate Indigenous traditional ecological knowledge, oral histories, and cultural practices into land use planning, restoration projects, climate adaptation strategies, and resource management decisions, supported by enhanced tribal consultation protocols and opportunities for co-management agreements on federal lands affecting tribal interests.

White House order setting enforceable sustainability performance targets across all federal buildings and campuses, specifying percentage reductions in energy intensity, water consumption, waste diversion rates, and embodied carbon in construction materials, with mandatory annual progress reporting, third-party verification, and integration into agency budgeting processes.

It is hereby ordered that federal acquisition and procurement policies shall give strong preference to domestically mined, processed, and manufactured critical minerals and materials essential for clean energy, defense, and advanced technology applications, including updated domestic content thresholds and incentives for supply chain reshoring.

Order by the President advancing equity, diversity, and inclusion in federal scientific research funding programs by requiring applicants to submit detailed DEI plans, mandating impact assessments on underrepresented groups, establishing participation benchmarks, and prioritizing awards that demonstrate meaningful broadening of participation from historically excluded communities in STEM fields.

Order requiring each federal agency to conduct comprehensive skills gap analyses in emerging technology areas such as artificial intelligence, quantum information science, cybersecurity, and biotechnology, followed by targeted recruitment, reskilling, and retention strategies to build a future-ready federal workforce capable of addressing national challenges.

White House order bolstering federal defenses against cyber threats targeting election infrastructure by mandating enhanced threat intelligence sharing, mandatory incident reporting timelines, regular resilience testing for state and local systems, and coordinated rapid response protocols across intelligence, law enforcement, and election administration agencies.

By the authority vested in me as President, federal agencies are directed to incorporate behavioral science research, evidence-based nudges, and human-centered design principles into the structure, communications, and delivery of public programs to improve participation rates, compliance, and overall effectiveness in areas such as public health, energy conservation, and financial decision-making.

Presidential directive establishing an interagency Ocean Health and Resilience Council to coordinate federal efforts aimed at reducing marine plastic pollution, ending illegal fishing, mitigating ocean acidification impacts, restoring coral reefs and coastal habitats, and integrating ocean considerations into climate adaptation and blue economy strategies.

Order mandating that federal health care delivery, public benefits, and social services programs routinely screen participants for social determinants of health such as housing instability, food insecurity, transportation barriers, and interpersonal violence, with required referrals to community resources and follow-up tracking as a core component of service delivery.

Presidential directive promoting the responsible and transparent use of generative artificial intelligence tools throughout federal agencies by establishing clear guidelines for disclosure of AI-generated content, verification of factual accuracy, mitigation of biases and hallucinations, human-in-the-loop review requirements, and safeguards against misuse in public communications, decision support, and citizen-facing services.

Proposals to fund a national apprenticeship expansion program specifically targeting underrepresented groups in high-growth industries such as clean energy, healthcare, information technology, and advanced manufacturing, with federal matching grants to employers for training costs, mentorship programs, and wage subsidies during the apprenticeship period to promote diversity and fill skills gaps in the workforce.

Measures to create tax credits and low-interest loans for employers establishing or expanding on-site child care facilities for employees, including dependent care for elderly family members, with requirements for licensed staff, safety standards, and affordability sliding scales to support working families and increase labor force participation rates among parents and caregivers.

Legislation establishing a federal paid sick leave insurance program that provides partial wage replacement for up to 7 days per year for personal illness, family care, or preventive health visits, funded through a shared payroll contribution, with job protection and anti-retaliation provisions to improve public health and worker security.

Initiative to launch a federal grant program for community solar projects in low-income and environmental justice neighborhoods, providing funding for installation, ownership models, and bill credits to reduce energy costs, promote local job creation, and advance equitable access to renewable energy.

Reforms to federal permitting processes for clean energy projects by implementing concurrent environmental reviews, statutory timelines for decisions, interagency coordination, and mandatory community benefit agreements to accelerate deployment while ensuring environmental protections and local input.

Package supporting regenerative agriculture practices through USDA incentives, including cost-share payments for cover cropping, no-till farming, rotational grazing, and soil health monitoring, with technical assistance and research funding to improve farm resilience, carbon sequestration, and water quality.

Effort to expand Medicare coverage to include comprehensive dental, vision, and hearing services for all beneficiaries, phased in over five years starting with low-income seniors, funded through savings from drug price negotiation and modest premium adjustments for high-income enrollees.

Provisions requiring large corporations to publicly disclose all political expenditures, including contributions to PACs, super PACs, and dark money groups, with detailed reporting to shareholders and penalties for non-compliance to increase transparency in corporate influence on elections.

An act to protect journalists and press freedom in the digital era by strengthening federal shields against compelled disclosure of sources, enhancing legal protections against SLAPP suits, and providing funding for digital security training and legal defense funds for reporters facing harassment or threats.

Measures funding the restoration of coastal wetlands, mangroves, and barrier islands to serve as natural buffers against storm surges, erosion, and sea level rise, with grants to states and local governments for planning, implementation, and long-term monitoring in high-risk coastal communities.

Legislation establishing a federal right to repair for consumer electronics, agricultural equipment, medical devices, and motor vehicles, requiring manufacturers to provide owners and independent repair shops with parts, tools, diagnostic software, and repair manuals at fair prices.

Initiative to create tax credits for homeowners and landlords investing in energy-efficient home retrofits, including insulation upgrades, efficient windows, smart thermostats, and home electrification systems, with bonus credits for low-income households to reduce energy bills and emissions.

Reforms strengthening federal antitrust enforcement against big tech companies by lowering merger review thresholds, creating presumptions against acquisitions that increase market concentration, and empowering state attorneys general to challenge anti-competitive practices.

Proposals investing in the replacement of aging lead pipes in drinking water systems nationwide, prioritizing schools, child care centers, and low-income neighborhoods with full federal funding for remediation, testing, and community education to prevent lead exposure health risks.

Package to support veterans with expanded healthcare benefits, including mental health counseling, substance use treatment, prosthetic services, and caregiver support programs, with streamlined claims processing and outreach to underserved veteran populations.

Effort establishing federal standards for workplace heat safety protections, mandating employers to provide shaded breaks, hydration, heat illness training, and emergency response plans in outdoor and indoor high-heat environments, with OSHA enforcement and penalties.

Measures expanding telehealth parity under Medicare and Medicaid to cover mental health services in rural and underserved areas, with reimbursement at in-person rates, broadband subsidies for patients, and provider training to improve access and outcomes.

Legislation creating a national commission on gun violence prevention, composed of experts in public health, law enforcement, and community violence intervention, tasked with delivering evidence-based policy recommendations within 18 months on background checks, red flag laws, and safe storage.

Initiative funding urban heat island mitigation through large-scale tree planting, green roof installations, and cool pavement technologies in vulnerable neighborhoods, with community involvement in planning to reduce heat-related illnesses and energy costs.

Reforms to federal campaign finance laws by introducing public matching funds for small-dollar donations, lowering contribution limits for special interest groups, requiring real-time donor disclosure, and prohibiting foreign-influenced corporations from election spending.

Provisions supporting tribal self-governance by increasing block grant funding for tribal courts, law enforcement, health clinics, and infrastructure projects, while strengthening federal-tribal consultation requirements on policies affecting sovereignty and lands.

An act requiring new fossil fuel power plants to install carbon capture and storage technology, with federal tax credits, grants, and regulatory support to facilitate deployment and reduce emissions from the energy sector.

Measures to reform the immigration court system by hiring additional judges, support staff, and interpreters to reduce case backlogs, providing legal representation for unaccompanied minors, and establishing alternatives to detention for low-risk individuals.

Package expanding apprenticeship opportunities in high-growth sectors like clean energy and advanced manufacturing with federal funding for employer partnerships, curriculum development, and wage subsidies during training.

Effort establishing a federal paid family leave insurance program funded by payroll contributions, providing up to 12 weeks of partial wage replacement for new child bonding, serious illness care, or personal medical leave, with job protection.

Measures protecting endangered species and their habitats through updated federal recovery plans, increased enforcement against poaching and habitat destruction, and grants for conservation partnerships with states and private landowners.

Initiative creating incentives for school districts to transition to electric school buses, with federal grants for vehicle purchases, charging infrastructure, and maintenance training to reduce emissions and improve air quality for students.

Reforms modernizing federal regulatory review processes by incorporating sustainability metrics, equity impact assessments, and public participation requirements in all major rulemaking to ensure balanced, inclusive decision-making.

Proposals funding enhanced pandemic preparedness with federal stockpiles of PPE, ventilators, and testing kits, expanded surveillance systems, and rapid response teams for emerging infectious diseases.

Measures supporting community policing initiatives with federal grants for officer training, mental health co-responders, and community engagement programs to build trust and reduce crime in high-need areas.

Presidential directive requiring federal agencies to achieve 100% clean electricity procurement for operations by 2030, with interim targets, reporting requirements, and preferences for on-site renewables and power purchase agreements.

Cabinet order establishing equity screening criteria for new federal financial assistance programs, mandating applicants to demonstrate how activities will address disparities based on race, income, geography, and disability.

It is hereby ordered that federal agencies prioritize nature-based solutions like green infrastructure and wetland restoration for flood risk reduction, water quality improvement, and habitat conservation in all relevant projects.

Presidential directive advancing responsible AI use in government by requiring algorithmic impact assessments, human review in high-stakes decisions, and public transparency reports on AI systems deployed in public services.

Order mandating every federal agency to develop and update annual climate adaptation plans identifying vulnerabilities, specific measures, timelines, and budgets aligned with national climate assessments.

Presidential directive eliminating single-use plastics in federal operations by 2030, including bans on plastic water bottles and foodware, with requirements for sustainable alternatives in procurement contracts.

By the authority vested in me, federal agencies must expand telehealth and remote service delivery models, with focus on rural and underserved areas, including broadband subsidies and digital literacy support for beneficiaries.

Order by the President strengthening antimicrobial resistance efforts with updated national action plans, increased surveillance funding, and requirements for federal health facilities to report data and adopt stewardship practices.
Order creating a Federal Task Force on Youth Mental Health to coordinate school-based services, digital platform safety regulations, and community prevention programs for adolescents.

Order by the President integrating equity considerations into federal emergency management, including disproportionate impact assessments and targeted resources for overburdened communities during disasters.

Cabinet order promoting circular economy principles in federal procurement by prioritizing durable, repairable, and recyclable products, reducing packaging waste, and requiring lifecycle assessments for major purchases.
